title: Enable pre-commit linting
body:
This should help use keep the codebase more consistent, and avoid sevel
whitespace related issues, or bad file permissions.

pre-commit allows us to easily introduce other linters in follow-ups,
like bashate.

Note: pre-commit tool does *not* install any git-hooks. Making commits
will will call the tool unless you deliverately tell it to install the
hooks.

comments.body:
@ssbarnea LGTM, but I'm not a yaml expert.  @cevich and/or @edsantiago if you've time to peruse, that would be great.

Also this has a WIP label, but I'm not seeing WIP in the title/description.  Should the label be removed @ssbarnea ?
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #4850) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
LGTM. Thanks for persevering on this.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago*, *ssbarnea*, *TomSweeneyRedHat* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [TomSweeneyRedHat] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

> "FATAL: pre-commit was not found, check https://pre-commit.com/ about installing it."

This just hit me. I'd wish for a make target that installs pre-commit as we do for all required tools.

@vrothberg Original proposal had that, but I was asked to remove it. I understood that there is a desire to auto-install only go build dependencies and not non-go ones. Still, feel free to reopen the subject because I agree that makefile should attempt to install it.
We want this under make install.tools, I believe, as opposed to automatically being installed on first use.
or if it's only something needed for automation, it can be installed by related scripts or in container images (e.g. the gating image).

The problem is that it got added to the default `make lint` target, so it's breaking developer environments. The solution is to create a new `.install.pre-commit` target and let developers install it if they so wish. (It would also be nice to make missing-pre-commit a warning, not fatal, but I've already said enough about that).
